questions.stem: Under ASC 606, which of the following is the correct order of the five-step revenue recognition model?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Identify contract, identify performance obligations, determine transaction price, allocate transaction price, recognize revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Identify performance obligations, identify contract, determine transaction price, recognize revenue, allocate transaction price

choices.label: C
choices.text: Determine transaction price, identify contract, identify performance obligations, allocate transaction price, recognize revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: Identify contract, determine transaction price, identify performance obligations, recognize revenue, allocate transaction price

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-05-4 outlines the five-step model: (1) Identify the contract with a customer, (2) Identify the performance obligations in the contract, (3) Determine the transaction price, (4) Allocate the transaction price to performance obligations, (5) Recognize revenue when (or as) each performance obligation is satisfied.
questions.explanationJa: 5ステップモデルは以下のとおりである: (1) 顧客との契約を識別、(2) 契約における履行義務を識別、(3) 取引価格を算定、(4) 取引価格を履行義務に配分、(5) 各履行義務が充足された時点で(または充足されるにつれて)収益を認識。

questions.stem: A software company sells a software license bundled with one year of technical support. Under ASC 606, how should the company account for these elements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize all revenue when the software is delivered

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize all revenue ratably over the support period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Identify two separate performance obligations and allocate the transaction price based on standalone selling prices

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer all revenue until the support period expires

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-14, a promised good or service is distinct if the customer can benefit from it on its own and it is separately identifiable. The software license and technical support are distinct performance obligations, and the transaction price should be allocated based on relative standalone selling prices per ASC 606-10-32-31.
questions.explanationJa: 約束された財またはサービスは、顧客がそれ単独で便益を得ることができ、かつ別個に識別可能である場合にdistinct(区別可能)となる。ソフトウェアライセンスとテクニカルサポートは別個の履行義務であり、取引価格は独立販売価格の比率に基づいて配分する。

questions.stem: Entity A enters into a contract to sell 1,000 units at $50 per unit with a volume discount of $5 per unit if the customer purchases more than 2,000 units within the year. Based on experience, Entity A estimates a 70% probability the threshold will be met. Using the most likely amount method, what is the transaction price per unit?
questions.explanation: Under the most likely amount method (ASC 606-10-32-8), the entity selects the single most likely outcome. Since there is a 70% probability the threshold will be met, the most likely outcome is that the discount applies, making the transaction price $50 - $5 = $45 per unit. The constraint on variable consideration must also be assessed to determine if this amount should be included.
questions.explanationJa: 最頻値法(most likely amount)では、最も可能性の高い単一の結果を選択する。閾値達成の確率が70%であるため、最も可能性の高い結果は割引が適用されることであり、取引価格は$50 - $5 = $45/単位となる。なお、この金額を含めるかは変動対価の制限(constraint)も評価する必要がある。

questions.stem: Entity B has a contract to provide 100 units of a product for $10,000. After delivering 60 units, the contract is modified to include an additional 30 units for $2,700 (which reflects the standalone selling price). How should Entity B account for this modification?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a separate contract for the additional 30 units

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a termination of the old contract and creation of a new contract for 70 units

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a cumulative catch-up adjustment to the original contract

choices.label: D
choices.text: Prospectively by combining the remaining obligations with the new units

questions.explanation: Per ASC 606-10-25-12, a contract modification is treated as a separate contract when: (a) the scope of the contract increases because of the addition of promised goods or services that are distinct, and (b) the price of the contract increases by an amount that reflects the standalone selling prices of the additional goods or services. Both conditions are met here.
questions.explanationJa: 契約変更は以下の場合に独立した契約として扱う: (a) distinctな財・サービスの追加により契約範囲が拡大し、かつ (b) 追加分の独立販売価格を反映する金額だけ契約価格が増加する場合。本問では両方の条件が満たされるため、追加30個は別契約となる。

questions.stem: Which of the following criteria, if met, requires revenue to be recognized over time under ASC 606?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity's performance creates an asset with alternative use to the entity

choices.label: B
choices.text: The customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits as the entity performs

choices.label: C
choices.text: The contract includes a significant financing component

choices.label: D
choices.text: The transaction price includes variable consideration

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-25-27 states that an entity transfers control of a good or service over time if one of the following criteria is met: (a) the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits, (b) the entity's performance creates or enhances an asset that the customer controls, or (c) the entity's performance does not create an asset with alternative use and the entity has an enforceable right to payment for performance completed to date.
questions.explanationJa: 以下の基準のいずれかを満たす場合、一定の期間にわたり支配が移転する(over time認識): (a) 顧客が便益を同時に受け取り消費する、(b) 企業の履行により顧客が支配する資産が創出・増価される、(c) 代替的用途のある資産が創出されず、かつ完了済み履行に対する強制力のある支払請求権を有する。

questions.stem: Company X arranges for Company Y to provide services to a customer. Company X collects $1,000 from the customer and remits $850 to Company Y. If Company X is acting as an agent, how much revenue should Company X recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-36 through 55-40, when an entity is an agent, it recognizes revenue as the net amount of consideration it retains after paying the other party. Therefore, Company X recognizes $150 ($1,000 - $850) as revenue.
questions.explanationJa: 企業が代理人(agent)である場合、他の当事者への支払後に保持する対価の純額を収益として認識する。したがって、Company Xは$150($1,000 - $850)を収益として認識する。

questions.stem: Entity C incurs $20,000 in incremental costs to obtain a contract with a customer. The contract period is 4 years. Under ASC 606, how should Entity C account for these costs?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expense immediately as incurred

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalize and amortize over the 4-year contract period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Capitalize and amortize over the expected customer relationship period if longer than the contract

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record as a reduction of the transaction price

questions.explanation: ASC 340-40-35-1 requires that an asset recognized for incremental costs of obtaining a contract be amortized on a systematic basis consistent with the transfer of the goods or services to which the asset relates. The amortization period may extend beyond the contract term if the entity expects to provide goods or services related to the contract beyond the initial term.
questions.explanationJa: 契約獲得の増分コスト(incremental costs)として認識した資産は、関連する財・サービスの移転と整合する体系的な基準で償却する。当初の契約期間を超えて関連する財・サービスの提供が見込まれる場合、償却期間は契約期間を超えることがある。

questions.stem: Entity D sells equipment for $121,000 with payment due in two years. The cash selling price is $100,000. The implicit interest rate is 10%. What amount of revenue should Entity D recognize at the time of sale?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-15, when a contract contains a significant financing component, the transaction price is adjusted to reflect the time value of money. The entity should recognize revenue at the cash selling price ($100,000) at the time of transfer. The difference ($21,000) is recognized as interest income over the two-year period.
questions.explanationJa: 契約に重要な金融要素(significant financing component)が含まれる場合、取引価格は貨幣の時間価値を反映して調整する。収益は移転時点の現金販売価格($100,000)で認識する。差額($21,000)は2年間にわたり利息収益として認識する。

questions.stem: Entity E sells 100 products at $100 each and estimates that 5% will be returned. What is the amount of revenue Entity E should recognize at the time of sale?

choices.label: C
choices.text: $9,500 with a refund liability of $500

choices.label: D
choices.text: $10,000 with a contra-revenue of $500

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-22 through 55-27, when a customer has a right of return, the entity recognizes revenue for the amount of consideration to which it expects to be entitled (excluding products expected to be returned). Entity E recognizes revenue of $9,500 (95 units × $100) and a refund liability of $500 (5 units × $100).
questions.explanationJa: 顧客に返品権がある場合、企業は権利を得ると見込む対価の金額(返品予想分を除く)で収益を認識する。Entity Eは$9,500(95個 × $100)の収益と$500(5個 × $100)の返金負債(refund liability)を認識する。

questions.stem: A media company grants a three-year license to use its intellectual property (a brand name) to a customer. The nature of the IP is such that it is dynamic and the licensor undertakes activities that significantly affect the IP. Under ASC 606, how should the licensor recognize revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the point in time when the license is transferred

choices.label: B
choices.text: Over time as the customer benefits from access to the IP

choices.label: C
choices.text: When cash is collected from the customer

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the end of the three-year license period

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-58 through 55-61, a license provides a right to access the entity's IP (recognized over time) if all three criteria are met: (a) the entity undertakes activities that significantly affect the IP, (b) those activities are not a transfer of a separate good or service, and (c) the customer is exposed to positive or negative effects of those activities.
questions.explanationJa: 以下の3基準すべてを満たす場合、ライセンスはIPへのアクセス権(right to access)として一定期間にわたり収益を認識する: (a) 企業がIPに重大な影響を与える活動を行う、(b) それらの活動が別個の財・サービスの移転ではない、(c) 顧客がそれらの活動の正負の影響にさらされる。

questions.stem: Alpha Corp. enters a contract for $450,000 that includes two performance obligations: delivery of equipment (standalone selling price $400,000) and installation services (standalone selling price $100,000). How much of the transaction price is allocated to the equipment?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-31, the transaction price is allocated based on relative standalone selling prices. Total SSP = $400,000 + $100,000 = $500,000. Equipment allocation = $450,000 × ($400,000 / $500,000) = $450,000 × 80% = $360,000. Installation = $450,000 × 20% = $90,000.

questions.stem: Beta Construction enters a fixed-price contract for $2,000,000. Estimated total costs are $1,600,000. By the end of Year 1, costs incurred to date are $640,000. Using the cost-to-cost input method, how much revenue should Beta recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the cost-to-cost input method: Percentage complete = Costs incurred / Estimated total costs = $640,000 / $1,600,000 = 40%. Revenue recognized in Year 1 = $2,000,000 × 40% = $800,000.

questions.stem: Gamma Corp. enters a contract with a base price of $1,000,000 and a performance bonus. There is a 60% probability of receiving a $200,000 bonus and a 40% probability of receiving no bonus. Using the expected value method, what is the transaction price (before applying the constraint)?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-8, the expected value method sums probability-weighted amounts. Expected bonus = (60% × $200,000) + (40% × $0) = $120,000. Transaction price = $1,000,000 + $120,000 = $1,120,000. This amount is subject to the variable consideration constraint (ASC 606-10-32-11).
questions.explanationJa: 期待値法(expected value method)は確率加重金額を合計する。期待ボーナス = (60% × $200,000) + (40% × $0) = $120,000。取引価格 = $1,000,000 + $120,000 = $1,120,000。この金額は変動対価の制限(constraint)の対象となる。

questions.stem: Delta Construction has a $5,000,000 fixed-price contract. Year 1: costs incurred $800,000, estimated total cost $4,000,000 (revenue recognized: $1,000,000). Year 2: cumulative costs incurred $2,400,000, revised estimated total cost $4,000,000. What revenue should Delta recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Cumulative percentage at end of Year 2 = $2,400,000 / $4,000,000 = 60%. Cumulative revenue through Year 2 = $5,000,000 × 60% = $3,000,000. Revenue recognized in Year 2 = Cumulative revenue - Prior recognition = $3,000,000 - $1,000,000 = $2,000,000.

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Epsilon Corp. delivers equipment and receives a note for $133,100 due on December 31, Year 2. The cash selling price of the equipment is $110,000 (implicit rate 10%). How much interest income should Epsilon recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Revenue is recognized at the cash selling price ($110,000) on delivery. The note receivable ($133,100) includes a significant financing component. Year 1 interest income = $110,000 × 10% = $11,000. Receivable end of Year 1 = $121,000. Year 2 interest = $121,000 × 10% = $12,100. Total: $110,000 + $11,000 + $12,100 = $133,100.

questions.stem: Zeta Construction has a long-term contract with the following balances: Revenue recognized to date $400,000; Progress billings to date $350,000; Cash collected $300,000. What is the contract asset (costs and recognized profits in excess of billings)?
questions.explanation: A contract asset arises when cumulative revenue recognized exceeds cumulative billings. Contract asset = Revenue recognized to date - Billings to date = $400,000 - $350,000 = $50,000. The difference between billings and cash collected ($50,000) is an accounts receivable, not a contract asset.
questions.explanationJa: 契約資産は、累積認識収益が累積請求額を超える場合に生じる。契約資産 = 認識収益累計 - 請求累計 = $400,000 - $350,000 = $50,000。請求額と回収額の差額($50,000)は売掛金であり、契約資産ではない。

questions.stem: Eta Corp. enters a $700,000 contract with three performance obligations. Product A has an observable SSP of $300,000 and Product B has an observable SSP of $250,000. Service C's SSP is highly variable and uncertain. Using the residual approach for Service C, what amount is allocated to Service C?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-34, the residual approach may be used when the SSP is highly variable or uncertain. Residual amount for Service C = Total transaction price - Sum of observable SSPs = $700,000 - $300,000 - $250,000 = $150,000.
questions.explanationJa: 独立販売価格が非常に変動的または不確実な場合、残余アプローチ(residual approach)を使用できる。サービスCへの配分額 = 取引価格合計 - 観察可能なSSPの合計 = $700,000 - $300,000 - $250,000 = $150,000。

questions.stem: Theta Corp. sells 1,000 units at $80 each (cost $50 per unit) with a right of return. Based on experience, 8% of units are expected to be returned. What gross profit should Theta recognize at the time of sale?
questions.explanation: Units expected to be kept = 1,000 × 92% = 920. Revenue = 920 × $80 = $73,600. COGS = 920 × $50 = $46,000. Gross profit = $73,600 - $46,000 = $27,600. Additionally, a refund liability of $6,400 (80 × $80) and a right-to-recover asset of $4,000 (80 × $50) are recorded for expected returns.

questions.stem: Iota Corp. has a contract to produce and deliver 500 custom parts for $200,000. As of year-end, 300 parts have been delivered and accepted by the customer. Using the output method (units delivered), how much revenue should Iota recognize?
questions.explanation: Under the output method (ASC 606-10-55-17), progress is measured based on outputs delivered relative to total outputs promised. Percentage complete = 300 / 500 = 60%. Revenue = $200,000 × 60% = $120,000.
questions.explanationJa: アウトプット法では、約束した総アウトプットに対する引渡済みアウトプットに基づき進捗度を測定する。進捗度 = 300 / 500 = 60%。収益 = $200,000 × 60% = $120,000。

questions.stem: Kappa Corp. has a 3-year service contract (single performance obligation satisfied over time) for $300,000 ($100,000/year). Year 1 revenue recognized: $100,000. At the start of Year 2, the contract is modified (not a separate contract; remaining services not distinct from those already transferred). Total revised price for the 3-year contract is $360,000. What revenue should Kappa recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-13(c), when remaining goods/services are not distinct from those already transferred, the modification is treated as part of the original contract with a cumulative catch-up. Cumulative revenue through Year 2 = $360,000 × (2/3) = $240,000. Year 2 revenue = $240,000 - $100,000 (Year 1) = $140,000.
questions.explanationJa: 残りの財・サービスが既に移転したものと区別できない場合、変更は当初契約の一部として累積的キャッチアップ調整(cumulative catch-up)で処理する。第2年末までの累積収益 = $360,000 × (2/3) = $240,000。第2年収益 = $240,000 - $100,000(第1年) = $140,000。

questions.stem: Entity A enters into two contracts with the same customer on the same day. Contract 1 is for the sale of equipment for $500,000, and Contract 2 is for installation services for $50,000. The selling price of the equipment was negotiated based on the customer also purchasing installation services. Under ASC 606, how should Entity A account for these contracts?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Account for each contract separately since they are legally distinct

choices.label: B
choices.text: Combine the contracts and account for them as a single contract

choices.label: C
choices.text: Account for Contract 1 only and treat Contract 2 as a warranty

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer all revenue until both contracts are completed

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-25-9 requires entities to combine contracts when they are entered into at or near the same time with the same customer (or related parties) and one or more of specified criteria are met, including that the consideration in one contract depends on the other. Since the equipment price was negotiated based on the installation purchase, the contracts should be combined.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-9は、同一顧客と同時期に締結された契約で、一方の対価が他方に依存する場合等は契約を結合することを要求する。設備の価格が据付サービスの購入を前提に交渉されたため、両契約を結合して単一の契約として会計処理する。

questions.stem: Alpha Corp. sells a product with a standard one-year warranty included in the price and offers an optional extended two-year warranty for an additional fee. How should Alpha classify each warranty under ASC 606?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both are assurance-type warranties

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both are service-type warranties and separate performance obligations

choices.label: C
choices.text: The standard warranty is assurance-type; the extended warranty is a service-type performance obligation

choices.label: D
choices.text: The standard warranty is a service-type performance obligation; the extended warranty is assurance-type

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-19 through 25-21, a warranty that cannot be purchased separately (standard warranty) is typically an assurance-type warranty accounted for under ASC 460. A warranty that can be purchased separately (extended warranty) is a service-type warranty treated as a separate performance obligation under ASC 606.
questions.explanationJa: 別途購入できない保証（標準保証）は通常、品質保証型（assurance-type）としてASC 460に従い会計処理する。別途購入可能な保証（延長保証）はサービス型（service-type）として、ASC 606の独立した履行義務となる。

questions.stem: Beta Corp. manufactures custom machinery for Customer Z. Customer Z requests that Beta hold the completed machinery in its warehouse due to space constraints. Beta has identified the machinery, it is ready for transfer, and Beta cannot use or direct the machinery to another customer. Which additional criterion must be met for Beta to recognize revenue on a bill-and-hold basis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The customer must have paid in full for the machinery

choices.label: B
choices.text: The customer must have a substantive reason for the arrangement

choices.label: C
choices.text: The machinery must be insured by the customer

choices.label: D
choices.text: The arrangement must last less than 90 days

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-55-83 specifies criteria for bill-and-hold arrangements: (1) substantive reason for the arrangement, (2) product identified separately as belonging to customer, (3) product currently ready for transfer, and (4) entity cannot use the product or direct it to another customer. The customer's space constraint constitutes a substantive reason.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-83のbill-and-hold要件：(1) 取決めの実質的な理由、(2) 製品が顧客のものとして個別に識別、(3) 製品が現在移転可能な状態、(4) 企業が製品を使用または他の顧客に振り向けられない。顧客のスペース制約は実質的な理由に該当する。

questions.stem: Gamma Corp. delivers goods to Retailer D under an arrangement where Retailer D can return unsold goods without penalty and Gamma retains the risk of obsolescence. Retailer D has no obligation to pay until the goods are sold to end customers. When should Gamma recognize revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the goods are delivered to Retailer D

choices.label: B
choices.text: When Retailer D sells the goods to end customers

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the return period expires

choices.label: D
choices.text: When Gamma ships the goods

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-80, indicators of a consignment arrangement include: the entity controls the product until a specified event occurs, the entity can require return of the product, and the dealer does not have an unconditional obligation to pay. Since all indicators are present, control does not transfer until the goods are sold to end customers.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-80の委託販売の指標：特定の事象まで企業が製品を支配、企業が返品を要求可能、ディーラーに無条件の支払義務がない。すべての指標が当てはまるため、エンドユーザーへの販売時まで支配は移転しない。

questions.stem: Delta Corp. sells a product for $100,000 with a forward agreement to repurchase the product in 12 months for $110,000. The repurchase price exceeds the original selling price. How should Delta account for this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a sale with a right of return

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a financing arrangement

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a lease

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a sale and recognize the gain of $10,000 upon repurchase

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-70, when an entity has a forward obligation to repurchase the asset at a price equal to or greater than the original selling price, the contract is accounted for as a financing arrangement. The entity continues to recognize the asset and records a financial liability for the $100,000 received. The $10,000 difference is recognized as interest expense over the 12 months.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-70により、企業が当初販売価格以上の価格で資産を買い戻す先渡義務を有する場合、金融取引として処理する。資産の認識を継続し、受領した$100,000を金融負債として計上する。$10,000の差額は12ヶ月にわたり支払利息として認識する。

questions.stem: A gym charges a nonrefundable joining fee of $500 and a monthly membership fee of $50. The joining fee does not relate to any transfer of a promised good or service but compensates for administrative costs. Under ASC 606, how should the gym account for the $500 fee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize as revenue when received since it is nonrefundable

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize as revenue over the expected membership period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize as a reduction of future revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record as a liability indefinitely

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-51, nonrefundable upfront fees that do not relate to the transfer of a promised good or service are advance payments for future goods or services. The fee should be included in the transaction price and recognized as revenue over the period during which services are provided (the expected membership period).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-51により、約束した財・サービスの移転に関連しない返金不能の前払料金は、将来の財・サービスに対する前払金である。料金は取引価格に含め、サービス提供期間（予想される会員期間）にわたり収益として認識する。

questions.stem: Publisher E licenses intellectual property to Customer F. The license is the predominant item in the contract. Customer F agrees to pay a 5% royalty based on its sales of products using the IP. When should Publisher E recognize royalty revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the license is granted

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the subsequent sale or usage occurs, or when the performance obligation is satisfied, whichever is later

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ratably over the license period

choices.label: D
choices.text: When cash is collected from Customer F

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-55-65A provides a sales-based or usage-based royalty exception for licenses of IP. Revenue is recognized at the later of (1) the subsequent sale or usage occurring, or (2) the performance obligation to which some or all of the royalty has been allocated has been partially or fully satisfied.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-65Aは知的財産のライセンスに係る売上高・使用量ベースのロイヤルティの例外を規定する。(1) その後の売上または使用が発生した時点と、(2) ロイヤルティが配分された履行義務が部分的または全部充足された時点のいずれか遅い方で収益を認識する。

questions.stem: On November 1, Year 1, Entity G receives $120,000 from a customer for a 12-month service contract beginning on that date. As of December 31, Year 1, what amount should Entity G report as a contract liability?
questions.explanation: Entity G has received $120,000 and has provided 2 months of service (November and December). Revenue recognized = $120,000 × 2/12 = $20,000. The remaining $100,000 ($120,000 − $20,000) represents the entity's obligation to provide future services and is reported as a contract liability (deferred revenue).
questions.explanationJa: Entity Gは$120,000を受領し、2ヶ月分のサービスを提供済み。認識済み収益 = $120,000 × 2/12 = $20,000。残りの$100,000（$120,000 − $20,000）は将来のサービス提供義務を表し、契約負債（繰延収益）として報告する。

questions.stem: Entity H enters into a contract with a performance bonus of $200,000 if a project is completed by a specified date. Based on experience with similar contracts, Entity H estimates a 70% probability of earning the bonus. Entity H applies the most likely amount method. Under the constraint guidance, how much variable consideration should Entity H include in the transaction price?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $200,000, but only if it is probable that a significant reversal will not occur

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-11, variable consideration is included in the transaction price only to the extent that it is probable that a significant reversal of cumulative revenue recognized will not occur when the uncertainty is resolved. Using the most likely amount method, the estimate is $200,000 (the binary outcome with >50% probability), but this amount is included only if the constraint is met.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-11により、変動対価は不確実性が解消された際に認識済み累積収益の重大な戻入れが発生しない可能性が高い範囲でのみ取引価格に含める。最頻値法では見積額は$200,000（確率70%）だが、制約要件を満たす場合にのみこの金額を含める。

questions.subtopic: Standalone Selling Price - Adjusted Market Assessment
questions.stem: Entity J sells Product X (standalone selling price: $80) and Service Y (no observable standalone price) together for $100. Entity J estimates the market would pay $40 for Service Y based on competitor pricing with adjustments for its own margins. What is the allocated transaction price for Product X?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-33, the adjusted market assessment approach estimates standalone selling price by evaluating the market and estimating the price customers would pay. SSP for Product X = $80, SSP for Service Y = $40, total SSP = $120. Allocated to Product X = $100 × ($80 / $120) = $66.67.
questions.explanationJa: 調整後市場評価アプローチでは、市場を評価し顧客が支払う価格を見積もる。Product Xの独立販売価格 = $80、Service Yの独立販売価格 = $40、合計 = $120。Product Xへの配分額 = $100 × ($80 / $120) = $66.67。

questions.stem: Entity K enters into a contract to provide daily cleaning services for 2 years. Each day of cleaning service is distinct and substantially the same. Under the series guidance in ASC 606, how should Entity K account for these services?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As 730 separate performance obligations (one per day)

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a single performance obligation satisfied over time

choices.label: C
choices.text: As two performance obligations (one per year)

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a single performance obligation satisfied at a point in time

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-14(b), a series of distinct goods or services that are substantially the same and have the same pattern of transfer is accounted for as a single performance obligation satisfied over time. Each day's cleaning is distinct, substantially the same, and transferred over time using the same measure of progress.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-14(b)により、実質的に同一で移転パターンが同じである一連の別個の財・サービスは、一定期間にわたり充足される単一の履行義務として会計処理する。各日の清掃は別個で実質的に同一であり、同じ進捗度の測定基準で移転される。

questions.stem: Entity L sells a product for $1,000 and grants the customer an option to purchase additional products at a 40% discount within the next year. The standalone selling price of the option, considering the likelihood of exercise and the discount, is $100. How should Entity L account for the option?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ignore the option since the customer has not exercised it

choices.label: B
choices.text: Allocate a portion of the $1,000 transaction price to the option as a separate performance obligation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record a contingent liability for the expected discount

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the option in the footnotes only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-42, a customer option to acquire additional goods or services at a discount that provides a material right is accounted for as a separate performance obligation. Entity L should allocate a portion of the $1,000 transaction price to the option based on relative standalone selling prices.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-42により、重要な権利を提供する追加の財・サービスを取得する顧客オプションは、独立した履行義務として会計処理する。Entity Lは$1,000の取引価格の一部を独立販売価格の比率に基づきオプションに配分する。

questions.stem: Entity M sells $500,000 of gift cards during December. Based on historical data, Entity M expects 8% of the gift cards will never be redeemed (breakage). Entity M expects to be entitled to the breakage amount and can reasonably estimate it. In Year 1, 60% of the gift cards are redeemed. How much breakage revenue should Entity M recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-49, when an entity expects to be entitled to breakage and can reasonably estimate it, breakage is recognized in proportion to the pattern of rights exercised. Total breakage = $500,000 × 8% = $40,000. Estimated total redemption = 92%. In Year 1, 60% redeemed out of 92% expected = 60/92 = 65.22%. Breakage in Year 1 = $40,000 × 65.22% = $26,087.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-49により、企業が未行使分の権利を得ると見込み合理的に見積もれる場合、権利行使のパターンに比例して認識する。非行使見込み = $500,000 × 8% = $40,000。推定総行使率 = 92%。Year 1行使率60% ÷ 推定行使率92% = 65.22%。Year 1の非行使収益 = $40,000 × 65.22% = $26,087。

questions.stem: A construction company enters into a contract to build a custom building. The contract involves land preparation, foundation, structural framing, and finishing. None of these services are separately identifiable because the company provides a significant integration service. How many performance obligations exist?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Four - one for each phase of construction

choices.label: B
choices.text: One - the combined output (completed building)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Two - pre-construction and construction

choices.label: D
choices.text: It depends on the contract terms

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-21(a), goods or services are not distinct when the entity provides a significant service of integrating those goods or services with other goods or services promised in the contract. The integration service means the individual construction activities are inputs to the combined output (completed building), resulting in a single performance obligation.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-21(a)により、企業が契約内の他の財・サービスと統合する重要なサービスを提供する場合、個々の財・サービスは別個ではない。統合サービスにより個々の建設活動は結合されたアウトプット（完成した建物）へのインプットとなり、単一の履行義務となる。

questions.stem: Entity N operates a subscription service at $100/month. Customers prepay for 12 months ($1,200). Based on history, 15% of customers cancel after 6 months and receive no refund. Entity N does NOT expect to be entitled to the breakage amount. How should Entity N account for the unexercised rights upon cancellation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize the remaining balance as revenue immediately upon cancellation

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the remaining balance as revenue proportionally over the remaining period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize the remaining balance as revenue when the likelihood of the customer exercising remaining rights becomes remote

choices.label: D
choices.text: Return the unearned portion to the customer

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-48, if an entity does not expect to be entitled to the breakage amount, the entity recognizes the unexercised portion as revenue when the likelihood that the customer will exercise its remaining rights becomes remote. This is the alternative approach when breakage cannot be reasonably estimated or is not expected.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-48により、企業が未行使分の権利を得ると見込まない場合、顧客が残存する権利を行使する可能性がほとんどなくなった時点で未行使分を収益として認識する。これは非行使分を合理的に見積もれない場合の代替的アプローチである。

questions.stem: Entity P sells 1,000 units at $50 each on December 15, Year 1. Based on historical experience, Entity P expects 5% returns. The cost of each unit is $30. What is the refund liability Entity P should record at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-23, Entity P should recognize revenue of $47,500 (950 units × $50), a refund liability of $2,500 (50 units × $50) for expected returns, and an asset of $1,500 (50 units × $30) for the right to recover products from customers. The refund liability is $2,500.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-23により、Entity Pは収益$47,500（950個 × $50）、返品見込みに対する返金負債$2,500（50個 × $50）、顧客から製品を回収する権利としての資産$1,500（50個 × $30）を認識する。返金負債は$2,500。

questions.stem: Entity Q provides consulting services to Client R. Instead of cash payment, Client R transfers 1,000 shares of its publicly traded stock (fair value: $25 per share at contract inception). How should Entity Q measure the transaction price?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the fair value of the shares when the services are completed

choices.label: B
choices.text: At the fair value of the shares at contract inception ($25,000)

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the par value of the shares

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the cost of services provided

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-21, noncash consideration is measured at fair value at contract inception. The transaction price for Entity Q's services is $25,000 (1,000 shares × $25 fair value at contract inception). Subsequent changes in fair value due to reasons other than the form of consideration are not reflected in the transaction price.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-21により、非現金対価は契約開始時の公正価値で測定する。Entity Qのサービスの取引価格は$25,000（1,000株 × 契約開始時の公正価値$25）。対価の形態以外の理由による公正価値の事後的変動は取引価格に反映しない。

questions.stem: Entity S, a consumer goods manufacturer, pays $50,000 to Retailer T for preferred shelf space in Retailer T's stores. Entity S does not receive a distinct good or service in exchange. How should Entity S account for this payment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As selling expense in the period paid

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a reduction of the transaction price (revenue)

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an intangible asset amortized over the shelf space period

choices.label: D
choices.text: As cost of goods sold

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-25, consideration payable to a customer is accounted for as a reduction of the transaction price unless the payment is in exchange for a distinct good or service. Since Entity S does not receive a distinct good or service from Retailer T, the $50,000 reduces Entity S's revenue.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-25により、顧客に支払う対価は、別個の財・サービスとの交換でない限り、取引価格の減額として処理する。Entity SはRetailer Tから別個の財・サービスを受け取らないため、$50,000はEntity Sの収益を減額する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 606, which of the following is a required category for disaggregation of revenue disclosures?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Revenue must be disaggregated by individual customer

choices.label: B
choices.text: Revenue should be disaggregated into categories that depict how the nature, amount, timing, and uncertainty of revenue are affected by economic factors

choices.label: C
choices.text: Revenue must be disaggregated by product line only

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disaggregation is not required if the entity has only one reportable segment

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-50-5 requires an entity to disaggregate revenue into categories that depict how the nature, amount, timing, and uncertainty of revenue and cash flows are affected by economic factors. Examples include type of good or service, geography, market or customer type, contract type, and timing of transfer.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-50-5は、収益及びキャッシュ・フローの性質、金額、時期、不確実性が経済的要因によってどのように影響を受けるかを描写するカテゴリーに収益を分解することを要求する。例として、財・サービスの種類、地域、市場・顧客タイプ、契約タイプ、移転の時期がある。

questions.stem: Entity U adopted ASC 606 using the modified retrospective method. Which of the following correctly describes this transition approach?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All prior period financial statements are restated

choices.label: B
choices.text: The cumulative effect of applying the new standard is recognized as an adjustment to opening retained earnings in the period of adoption

choices.label: C
choices.text: No adjustment is made; the new standard is applied prospectively only to new contracts

choices.label: D
choices.text: Prior period comparatives are restated, and any difference is recognized in other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-65-1(d)(2), the modified retrospective method recognizes the cumulative effect of initially applying ASC 606 as an adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings in the period of adoption. Prior periods are not restated, but additional disclosures are required comparing results under the new and old standards.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-65-1(d)(2)により、修正遡及法では、ASC 606の適用開始による累積的影響額を適用開始期間の期首利益剰余金の調整として認識する。過年度は修正再表示しないが、新旧基準の比較に関する追加開示が必要となる。

questions.stem: Tech Corp. enters a contract to deliver software, installation services, and 2 years of post-contract support (PCS) for a total price of $500,000. The standalone selling prices are: software $300,000, installation $50,000, and PCS $200,000. How much of the transaction price is allocated to the software?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-31, the transaction price is allocated to each performance obligation based on relative standalone selling prices (SSPs). Total SSP = $300,000 + $50,000 + $200,000 = $550,000. Software allocation = ($300,000 / $550,000) × $500,000 = $272,727. The discount of $50,000 is spread proportionally across all obligations.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-31により、取引価格は相対的な独立販売価格に基づいて各履行義務に配分する。合計SSP = $550,000。ソフトウェアへの配分 = ($300,000 ÷ $550,000) × $500,000 = $272,727。

questions.stem: Builder Corp. has a contract to construct a building for $1,000,000. After construction begins, the customer requests additional work that is distinct from the original scope. The modification price of $250,000 reflects the standalone selling price of the additional work. How should this modification be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a separate contract

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a cumulative catch-up adjustment on the existing contract

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a termination of the old contract and creation of a new contract

choices.label: D
choices.text: Prospectively as part of the remaining performance obligations

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-12, a contract modification is treated as a separate contract when both conditions are met: (1) the scope of the contract increases because of the addition of promised goods or services that are distinct, and (2) the price of the contract increases by an amount that reflects the entity's standalone selling prices. Both conditions are met here.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-12により、(1)追加される財・サービスが区別可能で契約の範囲が拡大し、(2)価格が独立販売価格を反映した金額で増加する場合、別個の契約として処理する。両条件を満たすため別契約。

questions.stem: Pharma Corp. enters a contract to sell a drug to a distributor. The contract price is $1,000,000, but a $200,000 rebate is payable if the distributor's sales volume exceeds a threshold. Pharma estimates a 70% probability that the threshold will be met and 30% that it will not. Using the most likely amount method, what transaction price should Pharma recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-8, when there are only two possible outcomes, the most likely amount method is more predictive. Since there is a 70% probability that the rebate will be owed, the most likely amount is $800,000 ($1,000,000 − $200,000 rebate). The expected value method would yield $860,000 (70% × $800,000 + 30% × $1,000,000), but the most likely amount method considers only the single most likely outcome.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-8により、結果が2つしかない場合、最頻値法がより予測に適する。リベート支払の確率70%なので、最頻値は$800,000（$1,000,000 − $200,000）。期待値法なら$860,000だが、最頻値法では最も可能性の高い単一の結果のみ考慮。

questions.subtopic: Contract Costs - Incremental Costs of Obtaining
questions.stem: Sales Corp. pays its salespeople a 5% commission on each new contract signed. The commission is paid only for new contracts, not renewals. A salesperson signs a 3-year contract worth $600,000. How should the $30,000 commission be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalized as a contract asset and amortized over 3 years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expensed immediately when paid

choices.label: C
choices.text: Capitalized and amortized over the expected customer relationship period if longer than 3 years

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deducted from the transaction price

questions.explanation: Under ASC 340-40-25-1, incremental costs of obtaining a contract (costs that would not have been incurred but for the contract, such as sales commissions) are capitalized if the entity expects to recover them. Under ASC 340-40-35-1, the asset is amortized on a systematic basis consistent with the pattern of transfer of goods or services. If the commission relates to a contract that is expected to result in renewals, the amortization period should be the expected customer relationship period, which may exceed the initial contract term.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 340-40-25-1により、契約獲得の増分コスト（契約がなければ発生しなかったコスト）は回収見込みがあれば資産計上する。更新が見込まれる場合、ASC 340-40-35-1により初回契約期間ではなく顧客との関係期間で償却する。

questions.subtopic: Satisfaction Over Time - Control Transfer Criteria
questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT one of the three criteria under ASC 606 for recognizing revenue over time?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits as the entity performs

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity's performance creates or enhances an asset that the customer controls as it is created

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entity's performance creates an asset with an alternative use to the entity, and the entity has an enforceable right to payment

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entity's performance does not create an asset with alternative use, and the entity has an enforceable right to payment for performance completed to date

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-27, revenue is recognized over time if ANY of the following criteria is met: (a) the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits, (b) the entity's performance creates or enhances an asset that the customer controls, or (c) the entity's performance does NOT create an asset with an alternative use AND the entity has an enforceable right to payment for performance completed to date. Option C incorrectly states the asset HAS alternative use, which is the opposite of criterion (c).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-27により、一定期間にわたる収益認識の3基準は：(a)顧客が便益を同時に受領・消費、(b)企業の履行が顧客が支配する資産を創出、(c)資産に代替用途がなく完了部分の支払請求権がある。Cは「代替用途がある」と誤記しており正しくない。

questions.stem: Music Corp. licenses a music catalog to a streaming service for 5 years. The music catalog represents the entity's completed works, and Music Corp. does not undertake activities that significantly affect the catalog during the license period. Is this a right to access (symbolic) or right to use (functional) intellectual property?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Right to use (functional IP) — recognize revenue at a point in time when the license is transferred

choices.label: B
choices.text: Right to access (symbolic IP) — recognize revenue over the 5-year license period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Right to access — because all IP licenses are recognized over time

choices.label: D
choices.text: Right to use — but recognize revenue over time because the license period exceeds one year

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-60, IP is classified as symbolic (right to access) when the entity undertakes activities that significantly affect the IP, and the customer is exposed to effects of those activities. If the entity does NOT undertake such activities, the IP is functional (right to use), and revenue is recognized at the point in time when the customer can use and benefit from the license. A completed music catalog with no ongoing activities is functional IP.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-60により、企業がIPに重大な影響を与える活動を行わない場合、機能的IP（使用権）であり、ライセンス移転時に一時点で収益認識する。完成した音楽カタログで継続的活動がないため機能的IP。

questions.stem: Under ASC 606, when can an entity elect NOT to adjust the transaction price for the effects of a significant financing component?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the period between payment and transfer of goods/services is one year or less

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the interest rate is below the market rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the customer pays in advance

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the contract duration is less than 3 years

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-18, as a practical expedient, an entity need not adjust the promised amount of consideration for the effects of a significant financing component if the entity expects, at contract inception, that the period between when the entity transfers a promised good or service to the customer and when the customer pays for it will be one year or less. This simplifies accounting for short-term financing arrangements.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-18により、実務上の便法として、企業が契約開始時に移転と支払の間の期間が1年以内と見込む場合、重大な金融要素の調整は不要。短期の金融取り決めの会計処理を簡素化するもの。

questions.stem: Consulting Corp. provides services under a fixed-fee contract. At the end of Year 1, Consulting has completed 60% of the work (measured by input method) but has only billed for 40%. The unbilled amount represents $100,000. How should this be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a contract asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: As accounts receivable

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a contract liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: As deferred revenue

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-45-3, a contract asset is an entity's right to consideration in exchange for goods or services that the entity has transferred to a customer when that right is conditioned on something other than the passage of time. Since the billing is contingent on further performance (not merely the passage of time), the $100,000 is a contract asset. If the right to payment were unconditional (conditioned only on time), it would be classified as a receivable.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-45-3により、対価の権利が時間の経過以外の条件に依存する場合、契約資産に分類する。請求が追加履行に依存しているため$100,000は契約資産。無条件の支払請求権であれば売掛金。

questions.stem: Manufacturer Corp. sells inventory to a customer for $500,000 and simultaneously enters a forward agreement to repurchase the inventory in 6 months for $520,000. The repurchase price exceeds the original selling price. How should this transaction be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a financing arrangement — no revenue recognized

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a sale with right of return

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a lease

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a regular sale with revenue of $500,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-70, when an entity has an obligation to repurchase an asset (a forward), and the repurchase price is equal to or greater than the original selling price, the transaction is accounted for as a financing arrangement. The entity does not derecognize the asset and records a financial liability of $500,000. The $20,000 difference between the repurchase and sale prices is recognized as interest expense over the 6-month period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-70により、買戻義務（先渡契約）があり買戻価格が販売価格以上の場合、金融取引として処理する。資産を認識解除せず、金融負債$500,000を計上。差額$20,000は6ヶ月にわたり利息費用として認識。

questions.stem: Auto Corp. sells a vehicle for $40,000 and offers an optional 5-year extended warranty for $3,000. The extended warranty is sold separately in the market. How should Auto Corp. account for the extended warranty?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Allocate a portion of the $40,000 to the warranty and recognize over 5 years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize $3,000 as a separate performance obligation and defer revenue over 5 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: Accrue estimated warranty costs at the time of sale

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize the $3,000 warranty revenue immediately at the time of sale

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-34, a service-type warranty that provides a service beyond fixing defects existing at the time of sale is a separate performance obligation. Since the extended warranty is priced separately ($3,000) and sold independently, it is a distinct performance obligation. The $3,000 is deferred and recognized as revenue over the 5-year warranty period as the entity satisfies the obligation over time.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-34により、販売時の欠陥修理以上のサービスを提供するサービス型保証は別個の履行義務。延長保証は個別に価格設定・販売されており、$3,000を繰延べて5年間にわたり収益認識する。

questions.stem: Construction Corp. has a $5,000,000 fixed-price contract. At the end of Year 1, costs incurred are $2,000,000, estimated costs to complete are $4,000,000, and total estimated costs are now $6,000,000. What should Construction Corp. recognize in Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Revenue of $1,666,667 and a total loss provision of $1,000,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Revenue of $2,000,000 and no loss

choices.label: C
choices.text: Revenue of $1,666,667 and an additional loss of $666,667

choices.label: D
choices.text: No revenue and a loss of $1,000,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-27 (over time recognition using cost-to-cost), percentage complete = $2,000,000 / $6,000,000 = 33.33%. Revenue = $5,000,000 × 33.33% = $1,666,667. Costs = $2,000,000. However, the contract is expected to generate a total loss of $1,000,000 ($5,000,000 − $6,000,000). Under ASC 605-35-25-45 (now within ASC 606 guidance), the entire anticipated loss must be recognized immediately. The loss recognized in Year 1 includes the excess of costs over revenue ($333,333) plus the remaining expected loss ($666,667) = $1,000,000 total.
questions.explanationJa: 原価比率法で進捗度 = 33.33%。収益 = $1,666,667。契約全体の予想損失$1,000,000は即座に全額認識する。Year 1の損失 = 費用超過$333,333 + 残余予想損失$666,667 = $1,000,000。

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for a contract to exist under Step 1 of ASC 606?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The contract has been approved by the parties and they are committed to perform

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity can identify each party's rights regarding goods or services to be transferred

choices.label: C
choices.text: The contract must be in writing

choices.label: D
choices.text: Collection of the consideration is probable

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-1, five criteria must be met for a contract to exist: (a) approval and commitment, (b) identification of each party's rights, (c) identification of payment terms, (d) the contract has commercial substance, and (e) collection is probable. A contract does not need to be in writing — ASC 606 explicitly recognizes that contracts can be written, oral, or implied by customary business practices.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-1により、契約の5要件は：(a)承認と履行の約束、(b)各当事者の権利の識別、(c)支払条件の識別、(d)商業的実質、(e)対価の回収可能性。契約は書面である必要はなく、口頭や慣行によるものも認められる。

questions.stem: Software Corp. sells a software license bundled with a mandatory implementation service. The software cannot function without the implementation. Are these two distinct performance obligations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because they are separately identifiable

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because the software and implementation are not distinct within the context of the contract

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, because the software has standalone functionality

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, because implementation services are never a separate obligation

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-21, a good or service is distinct if it is (1) capable of being distinct (the customer can benefit from it alone or together with readily available resources) AND (2) distinct within the context of the contract (separately identifiable from other promises). While software may be capable of being distinct, if it cannot function without the implementation service, they are highly interrelated and not separately identifiable within the contract — they form a single performance obligation.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-21により、財・サービスが区別可能であるには、(1)単独で便益を得られ、(2)契約の文脈で別個に識別可能であることが必要。ソフトウェアが実装なしに機能しない場合、高度に相互関連しており、単一の履行義務を構成する。

questions.subtopic: SSP Estimation - Expected Cost Plus Margin
questions.stem: Service Corp. cannot directly observe the standalone selling price of a customized service. Historical data shows the expected cost is $80,000 and the entity's typical margin on similar services is 25%. Using the expected cost plus margin approach, what is the estimated SSP?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-34, when an entity cannot directly observe the SSP, it may use the expected cost plus a margin approach. SSP = Expected cost + Margin = $80,000 + ($80,000 × 25%) = $80,000 + $20,000 = $100,000. This approach forecasts the expected costs of satisfying the performance obligation and adds an appropriate margin for that good or service.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-34により、SSPを直接観察できない場合、予想原価プラスマージン法を使用できる。SSP = $80,000 + ($80,000 × 25%) = $100,000。履行義務充足の予想原価に適切なマージンを加算する。

questions.stem: Retailer Corp. offers a loyalty program where customers earn 1 point per $10 spent. Each point can be redeemed for a $1 discount on future purchases. A customer spends $1,000 and earns 100 points. The SSP of the points is estimated at $80 (considering the likelihood of redemption). How much revenue should be recognized from the $1,000 sale?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-42, customer options that provide a material right (such as loyalty points) are treated as separate performance obligations. The transaction price ($1,000) is allocated based on relative SSPs. SSP of goods = $1,000 (assumed), SSP of points = $80. Total SSP = $1,080. Revenue from sale = ($1,000 / $1,080) × $1,000 = $925.93. The remaining $74.07 is deferred as a contract liability until points are redeemed or expire.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-42により、重要な権利を提供する顧客オプション（ポイント）は別個の履行義務として扱う。取引価格$1,000を相対的SSPで配分。商品 = $1,000 ÷ $1,080 × $1,000 = $925.93。残り$74.07はポイント行使まで契約負債。

questions.stem: Entity M enters a contract to sell goods for $100,000 to a customer with significant credit risk. Entity M determines it is NOT probable that it will collect the consideration. Entity M delivers the goods and receives a nonrefundable payment of $30,000. When should Entity M recognize revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize $100,000 revenue when goods are delivered

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize $30,000 revenue when received, as it is nonrefundable

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize revenue only when the collectibility criterion is met or the contract is terminated and payments are nonrefundable

choices.label: D
choices.text: Never recognize revenue from this contract

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-7, when the criteria for a contract under Step 1 are not met (including collectibility), consideration received is recognized as revenue only when (a) the entity has no remaining obligation to transfer goods/services and all or substantially all consideration has been received and is nonrefundable, or (b) the contract has been terminated and the consideration is nonrefundable. Until then, payments received are recorded as a deposit liability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-7により、ステップ1の要件（回収可能性を含む）を満たさない場合、受領した対価は(a)残余義務がなく対価の大部分が返金不能、または(b)契約が終了し対価が返金不能の場合にのみ収益認識。それまでは預り金として計上。

questions.stem: Travel Corp. arranges hotel bookings for customers. The hotel sets the room rate, accepts the credit risk, and is responsible for the guest experience. Travel Corp. earns a 15% commission. Travel Corp. collects $1,000 from the customer and remits $850 to the hotel. How much revenue should Travel Corp. recognize?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 until the customer completes the stay

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-39, the key indicator for principal vs. agent is whether the entity controls the good or service before it is transferred to the customer. Since the hotel controls the room (sets the price, bears credit risk, and is responsible for service), Travel Corp. is an agent. An agent recognizes revenue on a net basis — only the commission of $150 ($1,000 × 15%). Gross reporting of $1,000 would be incorrect.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-39により、本人対代理人の判定は、財・サービスの移転前に企業が支配しているかがポイント。ホテルが部屋を支配しているため、Travel Corp.は代理人。手数料$150のみを純額で収益認識する。

questions.stem: Builder Corp. has a contract to build 100 units for $10,000 each ($1,000,000 total). After delivering 60 units, the contract is modified to reduce the price of the remaining 40 units to $8,000 each. The modification does not add distinct goods. The standalone selling price of the remaining units is $9,500 each at the modification date. How should Builder account for the modification?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a separate contract for the remaining 40 units

choices.label: B
choices.text: Prospectively, by adjusting the transaction price and measure of progress for the remaining 40 units

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a cumulative catch-up on the entire contract

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a termination of the old contract and creation of a new contract

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-13(b), when the remaining goods or services are distinct from those already transferred, and the modification does not qualify as a separate contract (because $8,000 < $9,500 SSP), it is treated as a termination of the old contract and creation of a new one. Effectively, for remaining goods that are distinct, the entity accounts for the modification prospectively. Revenue for the remaining 40 units = 40 × $8,000 = $320,000 recognized as units are delivered.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-13(b)により、残余の財・サービスが移転済みのものと区別可能で、別契約の要件を満たさない場合、旧契約の終了と新契約の開始として処理する。残り40ユニットの収益 = 40 × $8,000 = $320,000を将来に向かって認識。

questions.stem: Biotech Corp. enters a licensing agreement with a $2,000,000 upfront fee and a $5,000,000 milestone payment contingent on FDA approval. Biotech estimates a 40% probability of FDA approval. Under the constraint guidance, how much of the milestone should be included in the transaction price?

choices.label: B
choices.text: $2,000,000 (milestone excluded due to constraint)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 for the milestone; only the $2,000,000 upfront fee

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-11, variable consideration is included in the transaction price only to the extent that it is highly probable that a significant reversal in the amount of cumulative revenue recognized will NOT occur. A binary outcome (FDA approval with only 40% probability) involves high uncertainty — if the milestone is included and FDA approval is not obtained, a significant revenue reversal would occur. Therefore, the constraint prevents inclusion of the $5,000,000 milestone until the uncertainty is resolved. Only the $2,000,000 upfront fee is included.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-11により、変動対価は累積収益の重大な戻入が起きない可能性が非常に高い範囲でのみ取引価格に含める。FDA承認確率40%の二値的結果は高い不確実性があり、マイルストーン$5,000,000は制約により除外。前払金$2,000,000のみを含める。

questions.stem: Publisher Corp. licenses its brand name to a retailer. The royalty is 5% of the retailer's sales. In Q1, the retailer reports sales of $2,000,000. When should Publisher Corp. recognize the $100,000 royalty revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the license is granted

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the subsequent sales occur (i.e., as Q1 sales are made)

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the end of the contract period

choices.label: D
choices.text: When cash is received from the retailer

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-65, for licenses of intellectual property, sales-based or usage-based royalties are recognized as revenue at the later of (1) when the subsequent sale or usage occurs, or (2) when the performance obligation to which the royalty is allocated has been satisfied. Since the license has been granted (obligation satisfied) and sales occurred in Q1, revenue is recognized as the underlying sales are made — $100,000 in Q1.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-65により、IP ライセンスの売上ベースロイヤルティは、(1)売上の発生時、または(2)関連する履行義務の充足時のいずれか遅い方で認識する。ライセンスは付与済みでQ1に売上発生のため、Q1に$100,000を認識。

questions.stem: Entity A has 5,000 nearly identical service contracts with individual customers. Each contract has the same terms and pricing. Under ASC 606, which of the following statements about applying the portfolio approach is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity must evaluate each contract individually regardless of similarity

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity may apply ASC 606 to a portfolio of contracts if it reasonably expects that the effect on the financial statements would not differ materially from applying ASC 606 to each individual contract

choices.label: C
choices.text: The portfolio approach is only permitted for contracts with a single performance obligation

choices.label: D
choices.text: The portfolio approach eliminates the need to identify performance obligations

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-10-4 permits an entity to apply ASC 606 to a portfolio of contracts with similar characteristics if the entity reasonably expects that the effect on the financial statements would not differ materially from applying the guidance to the individual contracts within that portfolio. This is a practical expedient, not a requirement, and it does not eliminate any steps of the five-step model.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-10-4は、類似した特性を持つ契約のポートフォリオに対してASC 606を適用することを認めている。ただし、ポートフォリオ単位で適用した場合の財務諸表への影響が、個々の契約に適用した場合と重要な差異が生じないと合理的に見込まれる場合に限る。これは実務上の便宜措置であり、5ステップモデルのいずれのステップも省略するものではない。

questions.stem: Entity B enters into two contracts with the same customer on the same day. Contract 1 is for consulting services at $50,000, and Contract 2 is for software implementation at $30,000 (which is below its standalone selling price of $45,000). The pricing of Contract 2 was negotiated in conjunction with Contract 1. Under ASC 606-10-25-9, how should Entity B account for these contracts?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Account for each contract separately since they are legally distinct

choices.label: B
choices.text: Combine the contracts and treat them as a single contract with a total transaction price of $80,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Combine only if the customer insists on a single invoice

choices.label: D
choices.text: Treat both contracts as a single performance obligation

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-25-9 requires entities to combine contracts entered into at or near the same time with the same customer if one or more of the specified criteria are met. Here, the consideration of one contract depends on the other contract (Contract 2 was priced below SSP because of Contract 1), satisfying the criterion in ASC 606-10-25-9(b). The combined transaction price is $50,000 + $30,000 = $80,000. Note that combining contracts does not automatically merge performance obligations — those are still evaluated separately.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-9は、同一顧客と同時期に締結した契約について、所定の基準を満たす場合に結合を求めている。本問では、Contract 2の対価がContract 1に依存して設定されており（SSPの$45,000を下回る$30,000）、ASC 606-10-25-9(b)の基準を満たす。結合後の取引価格は$80,000となる。なお、契約の結合は履行義務の統合を意味するものではない。

questions.stem: Entity C sells equipment to a customer for $200,000 and grants the customer a put option allowing the customer to require Entity C to repurchase the equipment for $170,000 at any time during the next 12 months. The expected market value at the end of 12 months is $140,000. Because the repurchase price is less than the original selling price, how should Entity C account for this arrangement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a lease under ASC 842

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a sale with a right of return

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a financing arrangement

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a consignment arrangement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-73, when a customer has a put option (the right to require the entity to repurchase the asset), the entity should consider whether the customer has a significant economic incentive to exercise that right. The repurchase price ($170,000) exceeds the expected market value ($140,000), so the customer has a significant economic incentive to exercise the put. However, because the repurchase price ($170,000) is lower than the original selling price ($200,000), ASC 606-10-55-74 requires the arrangement to be accounted for as a sale with a right of return under ASC 606-10-55-22 through 55-27.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-73に基づき、顧客がプットオプション（企業に買戻しを要求する権利）を有する場合、顧客がその権利を行使する重要な経済的インセンティブがあるかを検討する。買戻価格($170,000)が予想市場価値($140,000)を上回るため、顧客には行使のインセンティブがある。しかし、買戻価格($170,000)が当初販売価格($200,000)を下回るため、ASC 606-10-55-74により返品権付き販売として会計処理する。

questions.stem: Entity D sells inventory to a customer for $500,000 and simultaneously enters into a forward agreement obligating Entity D to repurchase the inventory for $530,000 in six months. How should Entity D account for this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize revenue of $500,000 at the time of delivery

choices.label: B
choices.text: Account for the arrangement as a financing arrangement, recognizing a financial liability of $500,000 and interest expense of $30,000 over six months

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize revenue of $530,000 when the repurchase occurs

choices.label: D
choices.text: Account for the arrangement as a sale with a right of return

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-70, when an entity has an obligation to repurchase an asset (a forward), control of the asset has not transferred to the customer. Because the repurchase price ($530,000) exceeds the original selling price ($500,000), the arrangement is accounted for as a financing arrangement per ASC 606-10-55-71. The entity recognizes a financial liability of $500,000 and accrues interest expense of $30,000 ($530,000 − $500,000) over the six-month period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-70により、企業が資産の買戻義務（フォワード）を有する場合、資産の支配は顧客に移転していない。買戻価格($530,000)が当初販売価格($500,000)を上回るため、ASC 606-10-55-71に基づき金融取引として処理する。企業は$500,000の金融負債を認識し、$30,000($530,000 − $500,000)の利息費用を6ヶ月にわたり計上する。

questions.stem: Entity E delivers customized machinery to a customer. The contract includes a customer acceptance clause stating that the customer must test the machinery and confirm it meets agreed-upon specifications before final acceptance. Entity E is uncertain whether the specifications have been met. Under ASC 606, when should Entity E recognize revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the time of physical delivery of the machinery

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the customer formally accepts the machinery after testing

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the acceptance period expires, regardless of customer action

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ratably over the acceptance testing period

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-55-86 states that if an entity is unable to objectively determine that the good or service transferred to the customer meets the specifications in the contract, then the entity cannot conclude that the customer has obtained control until the customer accepts the good or service. Because Entity E is uncertain whether the specifications have been met, revenue should not be recognized until the customer formally accepts the machinery.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-86により、顧客に移転した財・サービスが契約の仕様を満たしていることを企業が客観的に判断できない場合、顧客が受入れるまで支配が移転したとは結論づけられない。Entity Eは仕様が満たされているか不確実であるため、顧客が正式に受入れるまで収益を認識すべきではない。

questions.stem: Entity F sells goods FOB destination and ships them to customers. Under ASC 606, the entity elects the practical expedient for shipping and handling activities. Which of the following correctly describes the accounting treatment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Shipping and handling is always a separate performance obligation

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity may account for shipping and handling activities that occur after the customer obtains control as fulfillment costs rather than as a separate performance obligation

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entity must defer all revenue until the goods are delivered

choices.label: D
choices.text: Shipping and handling costs are offset against revenue

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-25-18B provides a practical expedient that permits an entity to account for shipping and handling activities that occur after the customer has obtained control of the good as fulfillment costs rather than as a promised service (i.e., a performance obligation). This expedient simplifies the analysis by avoiding the need to evaluate whether shipping and handling is a distinct service.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-18Bは、顧客が財の支配を獲得した後に行われる出荷・取扱活動を、約束したサービス（履行義務）ではなく履行コストとして会計処理することを認める実務上の便宜措置を提供している。この措置により、出荷・取扱が区別可能なサービスかどうかの評価を省略できる。

questions.stem: Which of the following is an indicator that an arrangement is a consignment rather than a sale, and therefore revenue should NOT be recognized upon delivery to the intermediary?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The intermediary has an unconditional obligation to pay for the goods

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity can require the return of the product or transfer to a third party

choices.label: C
choices.text: The intermediary has assumed all risks of loss

choices.label: D
choices.text: The intermediary has exclusive pricing authority with no return rights

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-55-80 provides indicators that an arrangement is a consignment. One key indicator is that the entity can require the return of the product or transfer the product to a third party. Other indicators include: the product is controlled by the entity until a specified event occurs (such as sale to an end customer), and the intermediary does not have an unconditional obligation to pay. Options A, C, and D describe characteristics of a sale, not a consignment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-80は、取決めが委託販売であることを示す指標を提供している。主要な指標の一つは、企業が製品の返品を要求できる、または第三者に移転できることである。その他の指標には、特定の事象（エンドカスタマーへの販売等）が発生するまで企業が製品を支配していること、仲介者に無条件の支払義務がないことが含まれる。選択肢A、C、Dは委託販売ではなく販売の特性を示している。

questions.stem: A SaaS company enters into a 3-year contract for $300,000 that includes: (1) cloud-hosted software access, (2) setup/implementation services, and (3) ongoing customer support. The standalone selling prices are: software $240,000, setup $60,000, and support $100,000. The setup services are not distinct from the software because the software requires significant customization. How should the $300,000 transaction price be allocated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $240,000 to combined software/setup obligation and $60,000 to support

choices.label: B
choices.text: $225,000 to combined software/setup obligation and $75,000 to support

choices.label: C
choices.text: $200,000 to combined software/setup obligation and $100,000 to support

choices.label: D
choices.text: $180,000 to combined software/setup obligation and $120,000 to support

questions.explanation: Because the setup services are not distinct from the software, they are combined into a single performance obligation under ASC 606-10-25-21. The combined SSP is $240,000 + $60,000 = $300,000. Support SSP is $100,000. Total SSP = $400,000. Allocation: Combined = ($300,000 / $400,000) × $300,000 = $225,000. Support = ($100,000 / $400,000) × $300,000 = $75,000. Total = $225,000 + $75,000 = $300,000.

questions.stem: A retailer sells $500,000 of gift cards during December. Based on historical experience, the retailer expects 8% ($40,000) will never be redeemed (breakage). By year-end, $150,000 of gift cards have been redeemed. Assuming the entity expects to be entitled to the breakage amount and recognizes breakage proportionally, how much breakage revenue should be recognized by year-end?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-49, when an entity expects to be entitled to a breakage amount, it recognizes breakage revenue in proportion to the pattern of rights exercised by the customer. The proportion redeemed = $150,000 / ($500,000 − $40,000) = $150,000 / $460,000 = 32.609%. Breakage revenue = $40,000 × 32.609% = $13,043 (rounded). Verification: the denominator uses expected redemptions ($460,000), and the numerator is actual redemptions ($150,000).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-49により、企業がbreakage（未使用残高）に対する権利を見込む場合、顧客が権利を行使するパターンに比例してbreakage収益を認識する。償還割合 = $150,000 / ($500,000 − $40,000) = $150,000 / $460,000 = 32.609%。Breakage収益 = $40,000 × 32.609% = $13,043（端数処理後）。分母は予想償還額($460,000)、分子は実際の償還額($150,000)を使用。

questions.stem: Entity G licenses software to a customer for $100,000 per year. The contract includes a renewal option for Year 2 at $100,000, which is the same as the standalone selling price. Under ASC 606, how should Entity G account for the renewal option?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize $200,000 in revenue at contract inception for both years

choices.label: B
choices.text: The renewal option does not represent a material right and is not a performance obligation; account for the renewal only if and when exercised

choices.label: C
choices.text: Allocate a portion of the Year 1 transaction price to the renewal option

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer all revenue until the customer decides whether to renew

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-42, an option to acquire additional goods or services at their standalone selling price does not provide a material right to the customer. Since the renewal price ($100,000) equals the SSP, the option does not need to be accounted for as a separate performance obligation. The entity accounts for the renewal only when the customer exercises the option, treating it as a new contract.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-42により、独立販売価格で追加の財・サービスを取得するオプションは、顧客に重要な権利を提供しない。更新価格($100,000)がSSPと一致するため、オプションを別個の履行義務として会計処理する必要はない。企業は顧客がオプションを行使した場合にのみ、新たな契約として処理する。

questions.stem: Entity H pays a sales commission of $24,000 to obtain a 3-year customer contract. The entity expects to renew the contract for an additional 3 years without paying a new commission. The total expected customer relationship period is 6 years. How much amortization of the capitalized commission should Entity H recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 340-40-25-1, incremental costs of obtaining a contract are capitalized if expected to be recovered. Per ASC 340-40-35-1, the asset is amortized on a systematic basis consistent with the pattern of transfer of the goods or services. Because the commission relates to the entire expected customer relationship (6 years, including the anticipated renewal), amortization per year = $24,000 / 6 = $4,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 340-40-25-1により、契約獲得の増分コストは回収が見込まれる場合に資産計上する。ASC 340-40-35-1により、財・サービスの移転パターンと整合する体系的な基準で償却する。コミッションは予想される顧客関係期間全体（更新を含む6年間）に関連するため、年間償却額 = $24,000 / 6 = $4,000。

questions.stem: Entity J sells industrial equipment for $800,000. The contract also includes a free equipment manual with a standalone selling price of $50. Under ASC 606, how should Entity J account for the equipment manual?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Identify the manual as a separate performance obligation and allocate a portion of the transaction price

choices.label: B
choices.text: The manual is immaterial in the context of the contract and need not be evaluated as a separate performance obligation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Defer revenue until the manual is delivered

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize the $50 as a separate line item of revenue

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-25-16A acknowledges that an entity is not required to assess whether promised goods or services are performance obligations if they are immaterial in the context of the contract. The manual's SSP of $50 relative to the $800,000 contract value is clearly immaterial, so the entity need not evaluate it as a separate performance obligation.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-16Aは、約束された財・サービスが契約の文脈において重要でない場合、履行義務としての評価を要求しないことを認めている。マニュアルのSSP($50)は契約金額$800,000に対して明らかに重要性がないため、別個の履行義務として評価する必要はない。

questions.stem: Entity K sells three products in a bundle for $180,000. The standalone selling prices are: Product X = $80,000, Product Y = $70,000, Product Z = $60,000. Entity K has observable evidence that the discount of $30,000 relates entirely to Product Z. How should the transaction price be allocated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Product X = $68,571; Product Y = $60,000; Product Z = $51,429

choices.label: B
choices.text: Product X = $80,000; Product Y = $70,000; Product Z = $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Product X = $60,000; Product Y = $60,000; Product Z = $60,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Product X = $72,000; Product Y = $63,000; Product Z = $45,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-36 through 32-38, a discount should be allocated entirely to one or more (but not all) performance obligations if the entity has observable evidence that the discount relates to those obligations. Because the entity has observable evidence that the entire $30,000 discount relates to Product Z, the allocation is: Product X = $80,000 (SSP), Product Y = $70,000 (SSP), Product Z = $60,000 − $30,000 = $30,000. Total = $80,000 + $70,000 + $30,000 = $180,000 (confirmed).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-36〜32-38により、値引きが特定の履行義務に関連するという観察可能な証拠がある場合、値引き全額をその履行義務に配分する。$30,000の値引き全額がProduct Zに関連する証拠があるため、配分は：Product X = $80,000、Product Y = $70,000、Product Z = $60,000 − $30,000 = $30,000。合計 = $180,000（検算済み）。

questions.stem: Entity L licenses its video game software to a customer. The software can be played offline without any updates. The license agreement does not require the entity to provide future updates, though optional DLC (downloadable content) may be purchased separately. Under ASC 606, how should the license be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a right to access the entity's IP (symbolic IP), with revenue recognized over time

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a right to use the entity's IP (functional IP), with revenue recognized at a point in time

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a combined performance obligation with any future DLC

choices.label: D
choices.text: Revenue recognition must be deferred until the license period expires
