questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-54, a license provides a right to use the entity's IP (functional IP) if the IP has significant standalone functionality. Software that can be used offline without ongoing updates has significant standalone functionality. Revenue for functional IP is recognized at a point in time when the license is transferred to the customer (ASC 606-10-55-58). The optional DLC is a separate future transaction, not a performance obligation in this contract.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-54により、IPが重要な独立した機能性を有する場合、ライセンスは企業のIPを使用する権利（functional IP）を提供する。オフラインでプレイ可能で継続的な更新を必要としないソフトウェアは重要な独立した機能性を有する。Functional IPの収益は、ライセンスが顧客に移転された時点で認識する（ASC 606-10-55-58）。オプションのDLCは別個の将来取引であり、本契約の履行義務ではない。

questions.stem: Entity M enters into a collaborative arrangement with Entity N to jointly develop and market a new drug. Under the arrangement, Entity M performs research activities and Entity N handles commercialization. Payments flow between the two entities. Under ASC 808, which of the following is correct regarding revenue recognition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All payments between collaborators must be recognized as revenue under ASC 606

choices.label: B
choices.text: ASC 606 applies to the portion of the collaborative arrangement where Entity N is a customer of Entity M

choices.label: C
choices.text: Payments between collaborators can never be recognized as revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entire arrangement must be accounted for as a joint venture

questions.explanation: ASC 808-10-45-4 requires that an entity in a collaborative arrangement apply ASC 606 to transactions with the other party if the counterparty is a customer for the distinct goods or services being provided. Revenue recognition depends on whether the other party meets the definition of a customer for the particular deliverables. If Entity N is receiving distinct goods or services (research output) from Entity M, and Entity N is a customer for those deliverables, then ASC 606 applies to that portion.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 808-10-45-4は、共同契約における相手方が、提供される区別可能な財・サービスの「顧客」に該当する場合、ASC 606を適用することを求めている。収益認識は、特定の成果物について相手方が顧客の定義を満たすかに依存する。Entity NがEntity Mから区別可能な財・サービス（研究成果）を受け取り、その成果物についてEntity Nが顧客である場合、その部分にASC 606が適用される。

questions.stem: Entity P has a contract where it has performed services worth $120,000 but has only billed and received $80,000 from the customer. There is no dispute about the amounts. How should the $40,000 difference be presented on Entity P's balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As accounts receivable

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a contract asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: As accrued revenue in other assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: As deferred revenue

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-45-3, a contract asset is an entity's right to consideration in exchange for goods or services that the entity has transferred to a customer when that right is conditioned on something other than the passage of time (e.g., the entity must perform additional obligations before billing). Because Entity P has recognized $120,000 in revenue but only billed $80,000, the $40,000 is a contract asset — the right to consideration is conditioned on further performance or billing milestones, not merely the passage of time.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-45-3により、契約資産とは、企業が顧客に移転した財・サービスの対価に対する権利で、その権利が時の経過以外の条件に依存しているものである。Entity Pは$120,000の収益を認識済みだが請求額は$80,000であり、$40,000は契約資産となる。対価に対する権利が追加的な履行や請求マイルストーン等の条件に依存しているためである。

questions.stem: Under ASC 606, which of the following is a required disclosure related to remaining performance obligations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The aggregate amount of the transaction price allocated to remaining performance obligations and an explanation of when the entity expects to recognize those amounts as revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the total number of unsatisfied performance obligations across all contracts

choices.label: C
choices.text: A detailed list of each individual contract's remaining performance obligations

choices.label: D
choices.text: Remaining performance obligations disclosures are optional under ASC 606

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-50-13 requires an entity to disclose the aggregate amount of the transaction price allocated to the performance obligations that are unsatisfied (or partially unsatisfied) as of the end of the reporting period. ASC 606-10-50-13a also requires the entity to explain when it expects to recognize those amounts as revenue, either on a quantitative basis using time bands or by using qualitative information.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-50-13は、報告期間末時点で未充足（または部分的に未充足）の履行義務に配分された取引価格の総額の開示を要求している。また、ASC 606-10-50-13aは、それらの金額をいつ収益として認識する見込みかを、時間帯を用いた定量的基準または定性的情報により説明することを求めている。

questions.stem: Entity Q estimated variable consideration of $50,000 at contract inception, but applied the constraint and included only $30,000 in the transaction price. At the end of Q2, new information indicates that the constraint should now include $45,000. What journal entry should Entity Q record in Q2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Accounts Receivable $15,000, Credit Revenue $15,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Contract Asset $15,000, Credit Revenue $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Contract Asset $15,000, Credit Deferred Revenue $15,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry is needed until the variable consideration is resolved

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-14, the transaction price (including the estimated amount of variable consideration) is updated at the end of each reporting period. The reassessment increased the constrained estimate from $30,000 to $45,000, resulting in an additional $15,000 of revenue. Because the right to the additional consideration is conditioned on something other than the passage of time (it is still an estimate subject to constraint), the debit is to Contract Asset, not Accounts Receivable. The credit is to Revenue as a cumulative catch-up adjustment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-14により、取引価格（変動対価の見積額を含む）は各報告期間末に更新する。制約後の見積額が$30,000から$45,000に増加し、$15,000の追加収益が生じる。追加対価に対する権利は時の経過以外の条件に依存する（依然として制約の対象となる見積り）ため、借方は売掛金ではなく契約資産となる。貸方は累積キャッチアップ調整として収益に計上する。

questions.subtopic: Output Method - Units Delivered vs Units Produced
questions.stem: A construction company uses the output method to measure progress on a contract. By year-end, the company has completed 40 of the 100 planned units. However, only 32 units have been delivered to and accepted by the customer. Under ASC 606, what percentage of completion should the entity use to recognize revenue?

choices.label: D
choices.text: 0% until all units are delivered

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-17, the output method recognizes revenue based on direct measurements of the value of goods or services transferred to the customer relative to the remaining goods or services promised. When using units delivered as the output measure, the entity should use the 32 units delivered to and accepted by the customer, not the 40 units produced. Therefore, progress = 32 / 100 = 32%.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-17により、アウトプット法は顧客に移転した財・サービスの価値を直接測定し、残りの約束された財・サービスとの比率で収益を認識する。引渡し済み単位数をアウトプット指標として使用する場合、生産済みの40単位ではなく、顧客に引渡し・受入れされた32単位を使用する。したがって、進捗度 = 32 / 100 = 32%。

questions.subtopic: Practical Expedient - Completed Contract at Transition
questions.stem: Upon adopting ASC 606, Entity S elected the practical expedient for completed contracts. Which of the following describes this expedient?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity need not restate contracts that were completed before the date of initial application

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity must restate all contracts from the last five years

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entity may apply the percentage-of-completion method to all historical contracts

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entity must defer all revenue until ASC 606 is fully adopted

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-65-1(f)(4) provides a practical expedient allowing entities to not restate contracts that begin and are completed within the same annual reporting period or that are completed contracts at the beginning of the earliest period presented. This significantly reduces the transition burden when adopting ASC 606 under the modified retrospective method.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-65-1(f)(4)は、同一年次報告期間内に開始・完了する契約、または表示される最も早い期間の期首において完了している契約について、遡及修正を不要とする実務上の便宜措置を提供している。これにより、修正遡及法によるASC 606適用時の移行負担が大幅に軽減される。

questions.stem: Entity T sells goods for a listed price of $200,000 but routinely offers price concessions to customers in this market segment. Based on historical data, Entity T estimates that it will provide a 10% price concession to this customer. Under ASC 606, what transaction price should Entity T use?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-7, price concessions are a form of variable consideration. The entity must estimate the amount of consideration to which it expects to be entitled. If Entity T routinely provides 10% price concessions, the estimated transaction price is $200,000 × (1 − 10%) = $200,000 × 0.90 = $180,000, subject to the constraint on variable consideration (ASC 606-10-32-11).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-7により、値引き（price concessions）は変動対価の一形態である。企業は権利を得ると見込む対価の金額を見積もる必要がある。Entity Tが通常10%の値引きを提供する場合、見積取引価格は$200,000 × (1 − 10%) = $180,000となる（変動対価の制約(ASC 606-10-32-11)の評価も必要）。

questions.subtopic: Incremental Costs of Obtaining - Amortization Period
questions.stem: Entity U capitalizes a $60,000 sales commission as an incremental cost of obtaining a 2-year contract. The entity uses the practical expedient in ASC 340-40-25-4 because the amortization period would be one year or less. Under which condition is this practical expedient available?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The practical expedient is available only when the contract term is one year or less

choices.label: B
choices.text: The practical expedient is available when the amortization period of the asset that the entity would recognize is one year or less

choices.label: C
choices.text: The practical expedient is available whenever incremental costs exceed $50,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: The practical expedient is available only for commission costs, not other contract costs

questions.explanation: ASC 340-40-25-4 provides a practical expedient allowing an entity to expense incremental costs of obtaining a contract as incurred if the amortization period of the asset that the entity otherwise would have recognized is one year or less. The key factor is the amortization period, not the contract term — the amortization period may differ from the contract term when renewals or expected customer relationships are considered.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 340-40-25-4は、認識されるはずだった資産の償却期間が1年以下の場合、契約獲得の増分コストを発生時に費用処理する実務上の便宜措置を提供している。重要なのは契約期間ではなく償却期間である。更新や予想される顧客関係を考慮すると、償却期間は契約期間と異なる場合がある。

questions.stem: Under ASC 606, which of the following is NOT an indicator of the transfer of control of a good at a point in time?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity has a present right to payment for the asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: The customer has legal title to the asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entity retains physical possession of the asset for its own use

choices.label: D
choices.text: The customer has accepted the asset

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-25-30 lists indicators of the transfer of control at a point in time: (a) the entity has a present right to payment, (b) the customer has legal title, (c) the entity has transferred physical possession, (d) the customer has the significant risks and rewards of ownership, and (e) the customer has accepted the asset. If the entity retains physical possession for its own use, that indicates control has NOT transferred to the customer.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-30は、ある時点での支配移転の指標を列挙している：(a)企業が現在の支払請求権を有する、(b)顧客が法的所有権を有する、(c)企業が物理的占有を移転した、(d)顧客が所有に伴う重要なリスクと経済価値を有する、(e)顧客が資産を受入れた。企業が自己使用のために物理的占有を保持している場合、それは支配が顧客に移転していないことを示す。

questions.stem: Entity V provides janitorial services to a building owner on a monthly basis. The customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits of Entity V's cleaning services as they are performed. Under ASC 606, how should Entity V recognize revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the end of each month when the invoice is sent

choices.label: B
choices.text: Over time, as the services are performed

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the end of the contract term

choices.label: D
choices.text: When cash is collected from the building owner

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-27(a), a performance obligation is satisfied over time if the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits provided by the entity's performance as the entity performs. Janitorial services are a classic example — the building is cleaned as the service occurs, and the customer receives the benefit immediately. Revenue is recognized over time as the cleaning services are rendered.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-27(a)により、企業の履行によって提供される便益を顧客が同時に受け取り消費する場合、履行義務は一定の期間にわたり充足される。清掃サービスはその典型例であり、サービスの実施と同時にビルが清掃され、顧客は即座に便益を受ける。収益は清掃サービスの提供に応じて期間にわたり認識する。

questions.stem: Entity W contracts to build a customized factory for a customer. The contract includes design, engineering, procurement of materials, and construction. These activities are highly interrelated and Entity W provides a significant integration service. Under ASC 606, how many performance obligations does this contract contain?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Four — one for each activity (design, engineering, procurement, construction)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Two — design/engineering combined and procurement/construction combined

choices.label: C
choices.text: One — the combined output of a completed factory

choices.label: D
choices.text: Three — design, procurement, and combined engineering/construction

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-21(c), a good or service is not separately identifiable if it is highly interrelated or interdependent with other goods or services in the contract. When an entity provides a significant integration service that combines various inputs into a single combined output, the individual activities are not distinct. Because the design, engineering, procurement, and construction are highly interdependent and Entity W provides a significant integration service, there is one performance obligation — the completed factory.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-21(c)により、財・サービスが契約内の他の財・サービスと高度に相互関連・相互依存している場合、別個に識別可能ではない。企業が様々なインプットを単一の結合されたアウトプットに統合する重要な統合サービスを提供する場合、個々の活動はdistinctではない。設計、エンジニアリング、調達、建設が高度に相互依存しており、Entity Wが重要な統合サービスを提供するため、履行義務は1つ（完成した工場）となる。

questions.stem: A telecommunications company enters into a contract that includes activating the customer's service, which involves administrative setup in the company's systems. The activation does not transfer a good or service to the customer. Under ASC 606, how should the activation activity be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a separate performance obligation with revenue recognized at activation

choices.label: B
choices.text: As an administrative task that is not a performance obligation

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a combined performance obligation with the ongoing service

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a contract fulfillment cost that should be capitalized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-17, activities that an entity must undertake to fulfill a contract but that do not transfer a good or service to the customer are not performance obligations. Activation is an administrative setup activity — it does not deliver a distinct good or service to the customer. Therefore, it is not a performance obligation. Any upfront fees charged for activation should be evaluated to determine if they relate to another performance obligation (such as the ongoing service).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-17により、契約を履行するために企業が行わなければならない活動であっても、顧客に財・サービスを移転しない場合は履行義務ではない。アクティベーションは管理的なセットアップ活動であり、顧客に区別可能な財・サービスを提供しない。したがって履行義務ではない。アクティベーションに対して課された前払料金は、他の履行義務（継続的なサービス等）に関連するかを評価する必要がある。

questions.stem: Entity X sells Product A on a standalone basis for $50 to similar customers in similar circumstances. Entity X also sells Product A in a bundle with Product B. When allocating the transaction price for the bundle, what standalone selling price should Entity X use for Product A?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50, because the observable standalone selling price is the best evidence of SSP

choices.label: B
choices.text: An estimated price using the expected cost plus margin approach

choices.label: C
choices.text: An estimated price using the adjusted market assessment approach

choices.label: D
choices.text: The residual approach, since Product A is always sold in a bundle

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-32, the best evidence of a standalone selling price is the observable price when the entity sells that good or service separately in similar circumstances to similar customers. Since Entity X sells Product A standalone for $50, this observable price should be used. Estimation methods (expected cost plus margin, adjusted market assessment, residual approach) are only used when the SSP is not directly observable (ASC 606-10-32-33 through 32-35).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-32により、独立販売価格の最良の証拠は、同様の状況で同様の顧客に対して当該財・サービスを個別に販売する際の観察可能な価格である。Entity XはProduct Aを単独で$50で販売しているため、この観察可能な価格を使用すべきである。見積方法（予想コスト+マージン法、調整市場評価法、残余アプローチ）はSSPが直接観察可能でない場合にのみ使用する（ASC 606-10-32-33〜32-35）。

questions.stem: Entity Y has three separate contracts with Customer Z, entered into at different times over a two-year period. Each contract was negotiated independently, has its own pricing, and does not reference the other contracts. Under ASC 606-10-25-9, should these contracts be combined?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes, because they are with the same customer

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes, because ASC 606 requires all contracts with the same customer to be combined

choices.label: C
choices.text: No, because none of the combination criteria in ASC 606-10-25-9 appear to be met

choices.label: D
choices.text: No, but only because the contracts were entered at different times

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-25-9 requires combination of contracts only when specific criteria are met: (a) contracts are negotiated as a package with a single commercial objective, (b) consideration paid in one contract depends on the other, or (c) goods or services in the contracts are a single performance obligation. Simply being with the same customer is not sufficient. Because these contracts were negotiated independently at different times with independent pricing, none of the combination criteria appear to be met.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-9は、特定の基準を満たす場合にのみ契約の結合を求めている：(a)単一の商業目的を持つパッケージとして交渉された、(b)一方の契約の対価が他方に依存する、(c)契約内の財・サービスが単一の履行義務である。同一顧客であること自体は十分ではない。これらの契約は異なる時期に独立して交渉され、独立した価格設定であるため、結合基準のいずれも満たされていないと考えられる。

questions.stem: Entity AA has a $1,000,000 construction contract recognized over time using the cost-to-cost method. The contract requires milestone payments: $300,000 at 25% completion, $300,000 at 50% completion, and $400,000 at completion. At the end of Year 1, costs incurred are $400,000 of estimated total costs of $800,000. Entity AA has received the first milestone payment of $300,000. What is the contract asset (or liability) balance at the end of Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Contract asset of $200,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Contract asset of $100,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contract liability of $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Contract asset of $500,000

questions.explanation: Percentage complete = $400,000 / $800,000 = 50%. Revenue recognized = $1,000,000 × 50% = $500,000. Cash received (milestone payments) = $300,000. Contract asset = Revenue recognized − Cash received = $500,000 − $300,000 = $200,000. The entity has recognized more revenue than it has billed/collected, resulting in a contract asset.

questions.subtopic: Progress Measures - Input vs Output Selection
questions.stem: Under ASC 606, which of the following factors should an entity consider when selecting a method to measure progress toward complete satisfaction of a performance obligation satisfied over time?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The method must always be the cost-to-cost (input) method for construction contracts

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity should select the method that best depicts the transfer of control of goods or services to the customer

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entity must use the output method whenever physical units are involved

choices.label: D
choices.text: The method should be chosen to maximize revenue recognition in earlier periods

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-55-17 states that in determining the appropriate method for measuring progress, an entity should consider the nature of the good or service that the entity promised to transfer. The objective is to select a method that faithfully depicts the entity's performance — that is, the transfer of control of goods or services to the customer. Both input methods and output methods may be appropriate, depending on the circumstances.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-17は、進捗度の測定に適切な方法を決定する際、企業が移転を約束した財・サービスの性質を考慮すべきとしている。目的は、企業の履行（すなわち顧客への財・サービスの支配移転）を忠実に描写する方法を選択することである。インプット法・アウトプット法のいずれも状況に応じて適切となりうる。

questions.stem: Entity BB has a $2,000,000 fixed-price construction contract. At the end of Year 2, the entity determines it can no longer reasonably measure progress toward completion. Costs incurred to date are $900,000, all of which are expected to be recoverable. Under ASC 606, what amount of revenue should Entity BB recognize cumulatively through Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-37, when an entity cannot reasonably measure the outcome of a performance obligation satisfied over time but expects to recover the costs incurred, the entity should recognize revenue only to the extent of costs incurred until such time as it can reasonably measure the outcome. This is sometimes called the zero-profit method. Total revenue recognized = $900,000 (equal to costs incurred).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-37により、一定期間にわたり充足される履行義務の成果を合理的に測定できないが、発生したコストの回収を見込む場合、成果を合理的に測定できるようになるまで、発生したコストの範囲でのみ収益を認識する。これはゼロ利益法と呼ばれることがある。認識収益合計 = $900,000（発生コストと同額）。

questions.stem: A contractor enters into a $5,000,000 construction contract. The customer furnishes materials costing $800,000 directly to the job site. The contractor has no control over these customer-furnished materials. Under ASC 606, how should the contractor account for the customer-furnished materials when measuring progress using the cost-to-cost method?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Include the $800,000 in both the numerator and denominator of the cost-to-cost calculation

choices.label: B
choices.text: Exclude the $800,000 from the cost-to-cost calculation entirely

choices.label: C
choices.text: Include the $800,000 only in the denominator

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize $800,000 as separate revenue when materials are received

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-21, when using an input method such as cost-to-cost, the entity should exclude the effects of any inputs that do not depict the entity's performance in transferring control of goods or services to the customer. Customer-furnished materials are not costs incurred by the contractor and do not represent the contractor's effort or performance. Therefore, they should be excluded from the cost-to-cost calculation entirely.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-21により、コスト対コスト法等のインプット法を使用する場合、顧客への財・サービスの支配移転における企業の履行を描写しないインプットの影響を除外すべきである。顧客提供材料は請負業者が発生させたコストではなく、請負業者の努力や履行を表さない。したがって、コスト対コスト法の計算から完全に除外する。

questions.stem: Entity CC sells electronics with a 30-day return policy. Customers who return goods are charged a 15% restocking fee. Entity CC estimates 10% of sales will be returned. During the period, Entity CC made sales of $1,000,000. What refund liability should Entity CC recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-23, an entity that expects returns should recognize a refund liability for the amount of consideration expected to be refunded. Expected returns = $1,000,000 × 10% = $100,000. However, the 15% restocking fee reduces the refund obligation: Refund per return = $100,000 × (1 − 15%) = $100,000 × 85% = $85,000. The refund liability is $85,000 because the entity only refunds 85% of the returned goods' purchase price. The $15,000 restocking fee is retained by the entity.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-23により、返品が見込まれる場合、返金が見込まれる対価の金額について返金負債を認識する。予想返品額 = $1,000,000 × 10% = $100,000。ただし、15%のリストッキング料が返金義務を減少させる：返金額 = $100,000 × (1 − 15%) = $100,000 × 85% = $85,000。返金負債は$85,000であり、企業は返品された商品の購入価格の85%のみを返金する。$15,000のリストッキング料は企業が保持する。

questions.stem: Entity DD receives a $240,000 advance payment on January 1 for a 2-year service contract. Services are provided evenly over the contract period. What journal entry should Entity DD record on January 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Cash $240,000, Credit Revenue $240,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Cash $240,000, Credit Contract Liability (Deferred Revenue) $240,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Cash $240,000, Credit Contract Asset $240,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Accounts Receivable $240,000, Credit Revenue $240,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-45-2, a contract liability is an entity's obligation to transfer goods or services to a customer for which the entity has received consideration. When Entity DD receives the $240,000 advance payment before performing services, it records Cash (debit) and Contract Liability / Deferred Revenue (credit). Revenue will be recognized as the services are performed — $10,000 per month ($240,000 / 24 months) over the 2-year period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-45-2により、契約負債とは、企業が対価を受領済みであるが、顧客に財・サービスを移転する義務を負っている場合に認識される。Entity DDがサービス提供前に$240,000の前受金を受領した時点で、現金（借方）と契約負債/前受収益（貸方）を計上する。収益はサービスの提供に応じて認識される（月額$10,000 = $240,000 / 24ヶ月）。

questions.stem: When adopting ASC 606 using the full retrospective method, which of the following is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity restates each prior reporting period presented in the financial statements as if ASC 606 had always been applied

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity records a cumulative catch-up adjustment to retained earnings at the beginning of the current period only

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entity applies ASC 606 only to new contracts entered into after the adoption date

choices.label: D
choices.text: Prior period financial statements are not restated; only footnote disclosures are updated

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-65-1(c), the full retrospective method requires the entity to apply ASC 606 retrospectively to each prior reporting period presented in accordance with ASC 250 (Accounting Changes and Error Corrections). This means restating comparative financial statements as if ASC 606 had been applied since the inception of each contract. Option B describes the modified retrospective method, not the full retrospective method.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-65-1(c)により、完全遡及法では、ASC 250（会計方針の変更と誤謬の訂正）に従い、表示される各過去の報告期間にASC 606を遡及的に適用する必要がある。これは、各契約の開始時からASC 606が適用されていたかのように比較財務諸表を修正再表示することを意味する。選択肢Bは完全遡及法ではなく修正遡及法を説明している。
